refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall provide worksite area analysis including terrain representation.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR includes multiple obligations: analyzing terrain, obstacles, and work-zone representation. To ensure atomicity and clarity, it is necessary to split these into separate sub-FRs, each focusing on a single aspect of worksite area analysis. This approach avoids conflating multiple requirements and maintains solution neutrality by focusing on what the system must do, not how.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verify that the system can generate and provide a representation of the terrain within the worksite area, independent of obstacle or work-zone data.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall provide worksite area analysis including terrain representation.
The NGHE solution shall provide worksite area analysis including obstacle representation.
The NGHE solution shall provide worksite area analysis including work-zone representation.

candidate_dps: Generate a digital terrain model representing the physical features of the worksite
Create a semantic map annotating terrain with relevant site features and attributes
Develop a modular worksite model combining terrain data with operational and safety overlays
Produce an abstracted spatial representation optimized for planning and execution modules
selected_dp.selected_dp: Develop a modular worksite model combining terrain data with operational and safety overlays

coupling_check.notes: The selected DP separates terrain representation from operational policies by modular overlays, supporting independence and reusability across planning and execution functions.

assumptions_risks: Assumes availability of accurate terrain data for model generation
Risk that operational policies may change frequently requiring updates to overlays
Assumes integration interfaces exist for planning and execution functions to consume the model
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Clarify the scope of 'site features' to ensure consistent inclusion in the model and define interface requirements for planning/execution functions to access the worksite model.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module encompasses the integration and processing of environmental and operational data to create a comprehensive modular worksite model, including terrain data and overlays for operational and safety information.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives real-time terrain and operational data from sensors and vehicles.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Sends safety-related alerts based on the worksite model overlays.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Facilitates data exchange with other system modules and external systems for model updates and dissemination.

module_containment_scores.notes: Highly cohesive as it directly manages the worksite model combining terrain and overlays; minimal diffusion as most data processing and model management is centralized here.
Low containment since vehicles primarily consume the model rather than build it; high diffusion if DP placed here due to need to share data with other modules.
Moderate containment as teleoperation may use overlays for operational context but does not build the model; diffusion is high if DP placed here.
Partial containment since safety overlays are relevant, but the full model including terrain and operational overlays extends beyond safety scope; moderate diffusion.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most specific and cohesive boundary for implementing the modular worksite model, as it integrates terrain data with operational and safety overlays, minimizing diffusion across other modules.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on timely and accurate telemetry data from vehicles and sensors.
Risk of interface complexity with multiple modules requiring access to the model.
Possible performance bottlenecks if the NGHE Solution module becomes a single point for all data processing.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP logically addresses the FR by specifying a modular worksite model that combines terrain data with operational and safety overlays, directly fulfilling the requirement for worksite area analysis including terrain representation.

diffusion_risks: Minimal diffusion risk as the DP is highly contained within the NGHE Solution module, which centralizes data processing and model management.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module has a high containment score (9) and low diffusion score (2), indicating strong cohesion and minimal scattering of the DP across multiple modules. Other modules have low containment and higher diffusion scores, supporting the choice of NGHE Solution as the implementation boundary.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible since the NGHE Solution module provides the integrated worksite model accessible to planning and execution functions, enabling validation against acceptance criteria such as model availability and inclusion of site features.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of responsibility to the NGHE Solution module, which owns the integration and processing of terrain and overlay data, ensuring traceability from FR through DP to IM.

recommended_revisions: Monitor and manage interface complexity to prevent bottlenecks due to multiple modules accessing the NGHE Solution module.
Mitigate risks related to dependency on timely and accurate telemetry data by implementing robust data validation and fallback mechanisms.
Consider performance optimization strategies within the NGHE Solution module to avoid it becoming a single point of failure or processing bottleneck.

decision.justification: Although the current FR, DP, and IM mappings are sound and pass all gates with strong containment and low diffusion risks, the high safety criticality of the system and the recommended revisions indicate the need for further detailed decomposition. Deriving next-level sub-FRs will enable more granular control over interface complexity, telemetry data validation, and performance optimization, which are crucial to mitigate identified risks and ensure robust verification and allocation clarity.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Acquire and validate telemetry data for terrain and overlays
FR2: Integrate terrain data with operational overlays into a unified worksite model
FR3: Provide real-time access to the worksite model for planning and execution modules
FR4: Monitor and optimize performance of the NGHE Solution module to prevent bottlenecks
FR5: Manage interfaces between NGHE Solution and other modules to maintain low diffusion and high containment

assumptions: The NGHE Solution module is capable of modularization to support sub-FRs.
Telemetry data quality and timeliness are critical for safety and system performance.
Interface complexity and performance bottlenecks pose significant risks in a high safety critical system.
open_risks: Potential bottlenecks due to multiple modules accessing NGHE Solution module.
Dependency on timely and accurate telemetry data.
Performance limitations leading to single point of failure.
evidence_needed: Detailed interface specifications and usage patterns.
Telemetry data validation and fallback mechanism designs.
Performance benchmarks and optimization plans for NGHE Solution module.
